name: when-documenting-code-use-doc-generator
description: Automated comprehensive code documentation generation with API docs, README files, inline comments, and architecture diagrams
capabilities: Analyzes code structure to identify functions, classes, and endpoints
Generates OpenAPI specifications and JSDoc/TypeDoc comments
Creates comprehensive README.md files from project metadata
Adds inline comments to undocumented code sections
Produces architecture diagrams using Graphviz or Mermaid
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Documentation generation skill with legitimate shell scripts for code analysis. All static findings are false positives or standard operations for documentation tools. Shell scripts use find/grep/xargs for code scanning, URLs are template references, and credential mentions are documentation only.
valueStatement: Manual documentation is time-consuming and often outdated. This skill automatically generates comprehensive API documentation, README files, inline comments, and architecture diagrams by analyzing code structure.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify runtime behavior
Requires readable source files in the project directory
Cannot verify documentation accuracy beyond syntax checking
Generated diagrams are based on static code analysis only

useCases.targetUser: Developers starting new projects who want documentation from day one
useCases.title: New Project Setup
useCases.description: Quickly generate initial documentation structure for a new project including README, API specs, and inline comments template.

useCases.targetUser: Maintenance developers working with undocumented code
useCases.title: Legacy Code Documentation
useCases.description: Add documentation to undocumented legacy codebases by analyzing existing structure and generating appropriate docstrings.

useCases.title: API Documentation Refresh
useCases.description: Update outdated API documentation by regenerating OpenAPI specs from current code endpoints.

promptTemplates.title: Generate API Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the documentation generator skill to create OpenAPI 3.0 documentation for this project. Look for Express.js, FastAPI, or NestJS routes and generate a complete API specification in docs/api.yml.

promptTemplates.title: Create Project README
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive README.md for this project. Include sections for overview, installation, usage, API reference, and contributing guidelines. Extract project name, version, and dependencies from package.json or pyproject.toml.

promptTemplates.title: Add Inline Comments
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the source code and add JSDoc comments to all public functions, classes, and interfaces. Include parameter types, return types, and descriptions where documentation is missing.

promptTemplates.title: Full Documentation Suite
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate complete documentation for this project including: (1) README.md with badges and table of contents, (2) OpenAPI specification for all endpoints, (3) architecture diagram showing component relationships, (4) inline comments for undocumented code.

bestPractices: Run documentation generation on fresh clones to avoid sensitive data exposure
Review generated documentation before committing to version control
Use the --overwrite flag cautiously as it replaces existing files
antiPatterns: Running documentation generators on production systems with live credentials
Expecting generated documentation to replace thorough code review
Ignoring documentation gaps identified by the analysis phase

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and other languages with detectable frameworks like Express.js, FastAPI, NestJS, and React.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my source code?
faq.answer: The skill only reads source files to analyze structure. It creates new documentation files without modifying existing source code.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format?
faq.answer: Yes, the shell scripts support options like --format for OpenAPI versions, --output for directory, and --no-badges for README generation.

faq.question: Where are documentation files generated?
faq.answer: Documentation is typically generated in a docs/ subdirectory or alongside existing files like README.md in the project root.

faq.question: Does this skill require network access?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes local files only. It references URLs in templates (like license URLs) but does not make network requests.

faq.question: What happens to existing documentation?
faq.answer: By default, existing files are backed up before modification. Use --overwrite flag to replace without backup.
